briefdescription.para: User configuration options.
Low level macros to read/write memory.
Macros to select INDAC and DAC modes.
Macros to select RX and TX transfer flags.
Macros to select Read and Write prototype.
Macros to select CS0 and CS1.
Macros to select different baud rate divisors.
Macros to select default OSPI configuration.
Macros to select different edge modes like SDR+NON-PHY, SDR+PHY and DDR+PHY.
Macros to select TX DLL delays.
Macros to select HWPIN and INBAND resets.
Macros to select DLL BYPASS and MASTER modes.
Macros to select SINGLE and STACKED connection modes.
Macros to enable/disable Dual Byte opcode.
The handler data type allows the user to define a callback function to handle the asynchronous processing for the OSPIPSV device.

detaileddescription.para: The application using this driver is expected to define a handler of this type to support interrupt driven mode. The handler executes in an interrupt context, so only minimal processing should be performed.

parameterdescription.para: Callback reference passed in by the upper layer when setting the callback functions, and passed back to the upper layer when the callback is invoked. Its type is not important to the driver, so it is a void pointer.
Holds one or more status events that have occurred. See the XOspiPsv_SetStatusHandler() for details on the status events that can be passed in the callback.

briefdescription.para: The configuration table for devices.
The configuration table for devices.
Asserts the chip select line.
De-asserts the chip select line.
Stub for the status callback.

detaileddescription.para: The stub is here in case the upper layers forget to set the handler.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the upper layer callback reference

briefdescription.para: Initializes a specific XOspiPsv instance so that the driver is ready to use.

parameterdescription.para: Reference to a structure containing information about a specific OSPIPSV device. This function initializes an InstancePtr object for a specific device specified by the contents of Config.

listitem.para: XST_DEVICE_IS_STARTED if the device is already started. It must be stopped to re-initialize.

briefdescription.para: Resets the configuration register.

detaileddescription.para: The Upper layer software is responsible for re-configuring (if necessary) and restarting the OSPIPSV device after the reset.

briefdescription.para: Reset the OSPI flash device.
Resets the OSPI flash device via OSPI controller.
Performs a transfer on the bus in polled mode.

detaileddescription.para: The messages passed are all transferred on the bus between one CS assert and de-assert.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the structure containing transfer data.

listitem.para: XST_DEVICE_BUSY if a transfer is already in progress.

briefdescription.para: Starts a DMA transfer.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the structure containing transfer data.

listitem.para: XST_DEVICE_BUSY if a transfer is already in progress.

briefdescription.para: Checks for DMA transfer complete.

listitem.para: XST_FAILURE if DMA transfer is not completed.

briefdescription.para: Performs a transfer on the bus in interrupt mode.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the structure containing transfer data.

listitem.para: XST_DEVICE_BUSY if a transfer is already in progress.

briefdescription.para: Handles interrupt based transfers.

listitem.para: XST_DEVICE_BUSY if a transfer is already in progress.

briefdescription.para: Stops the transfer of data to internal DST FIFO from stream interface and also stops the issuing of new write commands to memory.

detaileddescription.para: By calling this API, any ongoing Dma transfers will be paused and DMA will not issue AXI write commands to memory

briefdescription.para: Configures TX and RX DLL Delay.

detaileddescription.para: Based on the mode and reference clock this API calculates the RX delay and configure them in PHY configuration register.

briefdescription.para: Configures the dual-byte opcode mode.

parameterdescription.para: 1 to enable dual-byte opcode, 0 to disable.

briefdescription.para: Sets the status callback function, the status handler, which the driver calls when it encounters conditions that should be reported to upper layer software.

detaileddescription.para: The handler executes in an interrupt context, so it must minimize the amount of processing performed. One of the following status events is passed to the status handler.

para.preformatted: XST_SPI_TRANSFER_DONE The requested data transfer is done

detaileddescription.para:
InstancePtr
Pointer to the XOspiPsv instance.
CallBackRef
Upper layer callback reference passed back when the callback function is invoked.
FuncPointer
Pointer to the callback function.
None.
The handler is called within interrupt context, so it should do its work quickly and queue potentially time-consuming work to a task-level thread.

briefdescription.para: Looks up the device configuration based on the unique device ID/BaseAddress.

detaileddescription.para: The XOspiPsv_ConfigTable[] contains the configuration info for each device in the system.

parameterdescription.para: contains the base address of the device

simplesect.para: A pointer to the configuration found or NULL if the specified device ID/BaseAddress was not found. See xospipsv.h for the definition of XOspiPsv_Config.
In XSCT/classic flow, DeviceId is used to look up the device configuration.

briefdescription.para: Configures the clock according to the prescaler passed.

listitem.para:
XST_FAILURE if failed to set prescaler.
XST_DEVICE_IS_STARTED if the device is already started.
XST_DEVICE_BUSY if the device is currently transferring data. It must be stopped to re-initialize.

briefdescription.para: Used to signify the OSPIPSV driver that the Hardware flash configuration being used.

detaileddescription.para: This API should be called at least once in the application. If desired, it can be called multiple times when switching between communicating to different flash devices/using different configuration.

listitem.para: XST_DEVICE_IS_STARTED if the device is already started. It must be stopped to re-initialize.

simplesect.para: If this function is not called at least once in the application, the driver assumes there is a single flash connected to the lower bus and CS line.

briefdescription.para: Sets the options for the OSPIPSV device driver.The options control how the device behaves relative to the OSPIPSV bus.

detaileddescription.para: The device must be idle rather than busy transferring data before setting these device options.

parameterdescription.para: Contains the specified options to be set. This is a bit mask where a 1 indicates the option should be turned ON and a 0 indicates no action. One or more bit Values may be contained in the mask. See the bit definitions named XOSPIPSV_*_OPTIONS in the file xospipsv.h.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if options are successfully set.
XST_DEVICE_BUSY if the device is currently transferring data. The transfer must complete or be aborted before setting options.

briefdescription.para: Gets the options for the OSPIPSV device.

detaileddescription.para: The options control how the device behaves relative to the OSPIPSV bus.

simplesect.para: Options contains the specified options currently set. This is a bit Value where a 1 means the option is on, and a 0 means the option is off. See the bit definitions named XOSPIPSV_*_OPTIONS in file xospipsv.h.

briefdescription.para: Configures the edge mode (SDR or DDR).

parameterdescription.para: Edge mode. XOSPIPSV_EDGE_MODE_* represents valid values.

briefdescription.para: Configures how the controller will poll the device following a write transfer in DAC mode.

parameterdescription.para: Edge mode. XOSPIPSV_EDGE_MODE_* represents valid values.

briefdescription.para: Flash command based data reading using flash command control registers.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the structure containing transfer data.

simplesect.para: This operation is in IO mode of reading.

briefdescription.para: Flash command based data write using flash command control registers.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the structure containing transfer data.

simplesect.para: This operation is in IO mode of writing.

briefdescription.para: Reads the data using DMA.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the structure containing transfer data.

briefdescription.para: Reads the data using Linear controller.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the structure containing transfer data.

briefdescription.para: Writes the data Using Linear controller.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the structure containing transfer data.

briefdescription.para: Performs RX Tuning for SDR/DDR mode to calculate RX DLL Delay.
Configures the Rx and Tx taps in Phy Configuration register.
Checks for OSPI idle which means Serial interface and low level SPI pipeline is IDLE.
Writes data in Non-DMA Indirect mode.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the structure containing transfer data.

simplesect.para:
- XST_SUCCESS if successful.
XST_FAILURE if fails.

briefdescription.para: Disables the OSPIPSV device.
Enables the OSPIPSV device.
Configures the OSPI MUX to Linear mode.
Configures the OSPI MUX to DMA mode.
Reads data from RX FIFO.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the structure containing transfer data.

simplesect.para: This operation is in IO mode of reading.

briefdescription.para: Writes data to TX FIFO.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the structure containing transfer data.

simplesect.para: This operation is in IO mode of writing.

memberdef.argsstring: (const XOspiPsv *InstancePtr, u32 Cmd_op, u32 Rd_data_en, u32 Num_rd_data_bytes, u32 Cmd_addr_en, u32 Mode_bit_en, u32 Num_addr_bytes, u32 Wr_data_en, u32 Num_wr_data_bytes, u32 Dummy, u32 Membank_en)
memberdef.name: XOspiPsv_Setup_Stig_Ctrl

briefdescription.para: Configures the STIG control register (Flash cmd register).

parameterdescription.para: Specifies whether cmd_op requires data to read or not.
Specifies whether cmd_op requires addr or not.
Specifies whether cmd_op requires write enable or not.

briefdescription.para: Configures the below info to write instruction register DataXfertype - SPI/Dual/Quad/Octal AddrXfertype - 3 or 4B.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the structure containing transfer data.

simplesect.para: Used in DMA or Linear operations.

briefdescription.para: Configures the below info to read instruction register DataXfertype - SPI/Dual/Quad/Octal AddrXfertype - 3 or 4B.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the structure containing transfer data.

simplesect.para: Used in DMA or Linear operations

briefdescription.para: Sets the device size config register.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the structure containing transfer data.

briefdescription.para: Initiates the indirect read transfer.
Configures the Indirect controller.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the structure containing transfer data.

briefdescription.para: Sets up the Dma configuration.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the structure containing transfer data.

briefdescription.para: Initiates the DMA transfer.
Executes the Flash command configured using Flash Command control register.

simplesect.para: Wait till the command is executed.

briefdescription.para: Waits for bit to be set.

detaileddescription.para: This API polls for the required bit for 10 seconds, if not set then timeout occurs.

parameterdescription.para: Bit mask to check for lock.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if lock bit is set.

memberdef.argsstring: (XOspiPsv *InstancePtr, XOspiPsv_Msg *FlashMsg, u8 *AvgRXTap, u8 *MaxWindowSize, u8 DummyIncr, u32 TXTap)
memberdef.name: XOspiPsv_CalculateRxTap

briefdescription.para: Calculates the Max window size and the corresponding Average Rx Tap.

parameterdescription.para: Tx tap value used while doing Rx tuning.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if lock bit is set.

briefdescription.para: This function performs a polled data receive operation.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the structure containing transfer data.

briefdescription.para: This function performs a polled data send operation.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the structure containing transfer data.

briefdescription.para: This function performs an interrupt-based data receive operation.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the structure containing transfer data.

briefdescription.para: This function performs an interrupt-based data send operation.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the structure containing transfer data.

briefdescription.para: Silicon version.
Read Id opcode.
< prevent circular inclusions

detaileddescription.para: by using protection macros

briefdescription.para: < Macro used for more than 32-bit address
Minimum frequency for Tap grain selection.
< Maximum delay count
Max INDAC delay count.
< prevent circular inclusions

detaileddescription.para: by using protection macros

briefdescription.para: Max STIG delay count.
Max DMA delay count.
Max LOCK delay count.
Macro for 10^12.
Total number of configuration options.
The xospipsv.c file implements the functions required to use the OSPIPSV hardware to perform a transfer.

detaileddescription.para: This file contaions the data transfer functions for the OSPIPSV driver, including polled and interrupt-based send/receive operations.
The xospipsv_sinit.c file contains implementation of the XOspiPsv component's static initialization functionality.
The xospipsv_options.c file implements functions to configure the OSPIPSV component, specifically some optional settings, clock and flash related information.
The xospipsv_hw.h file contains low level access functions using the base address directly without an instance.
The xospipsv_hw.c file implements the hardware functions used by the functions in xospipsv_control.c file.
The xospipsv_g.c contains a configuration table that specifies the configuration of OSPI devices in the system.
The xospipsv_control.h header file for the low-level functions of OSPIPSV driver.
The xospipsv_control.c file implements the low level functions used by the functions in xospipsv.c and xospipsv_options.c files.
These are accessible to the user via XOspiPsv.h.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 nsk 02/19/18 First release sk 01/09/19 Added interrupt mode support. Remove STIG/DMA mode selection by the user, driver will take care of operating in DMA/STIG based on command. Added support for unaligned byte count read. sk 02/04/19 Added support for SDR+PHY and DDR+PHY modes. sk 02/07/19 Added OSPI Idling sequence. 1.1 sk 07/22/19 Added RX Tuning algorithm for SDR and DDR modes. 1.1 mus 07/31/19 Added CCI support at EL1 NS sk 08/08/19 Added flash device reset support. 1.2 sk 02/03/20 Added APIs for non-blocking transfer support. sk 02/20/20 Reorganize the source code, enable the interrupts by default and updated XOspiPsv_DeviceReset() API with masked data writes. sk 02/20/20 Make XOspiPsv_SetDllDelay() API as user API. sk 02/20/20 Added support for DLL Master mode. 1.3 sk 10/06/20 Clear the ISR for polled mode transfers. 1.4 sk 02/18/21 Added support for Dual byte opcode. sk 05/07/21 Fixed MISRAC violations. 1.5 sk 08/17/21 Added DCache invalidate after non-blocking DMA read. 1.6 sk 02/07/22 Replaced driver version in addtogroup with Overview. 1.8 sk 11/11/22 Enable Master DLL mode by default for Versal Net. sk 11/29/22 Added support for Indirect Non-Dma write. 1.9 sb 09/06/23 Fixed MISRAC violations. 1.10 akm 01/31/24 Use OSPI controller reset for resetting flash device. 1.10 akm 02/06/24 Increase the delay after device reset. 1.10 sb 02/09/24 Add support for Infineon flash part S28HS02G. 1.11 ng 08/20/24 Add spartanup device support 1.12 sb 01/28/25 Use stig read for byte count less than 8bytes. 1.13 sb 06/26/25 Set the required configurations for SpartanUp in SDR-PHY & DDR-PHY mode. 1.13 sb 10/28/25 Added cache invalidate when EL1_NONSECURE is not defined.

Ver Who Date Changes
1.2 sk 02/20/20 First release 1.3 sk 04/09/20 Added support for 64-bit address read from 32-bit proc. 1.4 sk 02/18/21 Added support for Dual byte opcode. sk 02/18/21 Updated RX Tuning algorithm for Master DLL mode. 1.6 sk 02/07/22 Replaced driver version in addtogroup with Overview. 1.8 sk 11/29/22 Added support for Indirect Non-Dma write. 1.8 akm 01/03/23 Use Xil_WaitForEvent() API for register bit polling. 1.9 sb 26/04/23 Updated address calculation logic in DAC read and write API's 1.13 sb 10/28/25 Added cache invalidate when EL1_NONSECURE is not defined.

detaileddescription.para: These functions will be used internally by the user API's.

para.preformatted: Ver Who Date Changes -- ----- --------------------------------------------. 1.2 sk 02/20/20 First release 1.6 sk 02/07/22 Replaced driver version in addtogroup with Overview. 1.8 sk 11/29/22 Added support for Indirect Non-Dma write. 1.8 akm 01/03/23 Use Xil_WaitForEvent() API for register bit polling. 1.9 sb 26/04/23 Updated address calculation logic in DAC read and write API's 1.11 ng 08/20/24 Add spartanup device support
Ver Who Date Changes -- ----- --------------------------------------------. 1.0 sk 01/09/19 First release 1.6 sk 02/07/22 Replaced driver version in addtogroup with Overview.

Ver Who Date Changes
1.2 sk 02/20/20 First release 1.3 sk 04/09/20 Added support for 64-bit address read from 32-bit proc. sk 08/19/20 Reduced the usleep delay while checking transfer done. 1.4 sk 02/18/21 Added support for Dual byte opcode. 1.6 sk 02/07/22 Replaced driver version in addtogroup with Overview. 1.8 akm 01/03/23 Use Xil_WaitForEvent() API for register bit polling. 1.11 akm 05/15/24 Added support for x2/x4 operations. 1.13 sb 10/28/25 Added cache invalidate when EL1_NONSECURE is not defined.

Ver Who Date Changes -- ----- --------------------------------------------. 1.0 nsk 02/19/18 First release sk 02/04/19 Added support for SDR+PHY and DDR+PHY modes. 1.6 sk 02/07/22 Replaced driver version in addtogroup with Overview. 1.8 akm 01/03/23 Use Xil_WaitForEvent() API for register bit polling. 1.11 ng 08/20/24 Add spartanup device support

Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 nsk 02/19/18 First release sk 01/09/19 Updated XOspiPsv_SetOptions() API to support DAC mode switching. Removed Legacy/STIG mode option in OptionsTable. sk 02/04/19 Added support for SDR+PHY and DDR+PHY modes. 1.1 sk 07/22/19 Added RX Tuning algorithm for SDR and DDR modes. 1.6 sk 02/07/22 Replaced driver version in addtogroup with Overview. 1.11 sb 07/09/24 Limit dummy value range in XOspiPsv_ConfigureAutoPolling(). 1.11 ng 08/20/24 Add spartanup device support

Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 hk 02/19/18 First release 1.6 sk 02/07/22 Replaced driver version in addtogroup with Overview. 1.9 sb 06/06/23 Added support for system device-tree flow. 1.14 vlt 12/15/25 Update Doxygen comments to include SDT flow details.

detaileddescription.para: These functions are used internally by the driver.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.13 sb 02/07/25 First release
